goal: Render solver-conditioned river surfaces continuously on the custom C++ solver-owned procedural ribbon while retaining failed Single Layer Water candidates as review evidence.

candidate_materials.source: RaftSim purpose-built Single Layer Water evaluation parent

test_run.build: SmokeEmIfYouGotEmEditor Mac Development succeeded with UE_5.8

observed_failure.summary: The engine WaterAdvanced material candidate produced a near-black river surface on the generated procedural river ribbon, making the downstream river-eye captures worse than the active vertex-color water preview.
likely_causes: plugin water material expects WaterBody or WaterZone data not present on the generated ProceduralMeshComponent ribbon
default plugin material parameters are not tuned for the custom preview mesh, manual exposure, or source-mask-driven river colors
the generated mesh currently needs a purpose-built material or Water plugin integration rather than a direct material swap
blocked_use: do not use the tested engine water material candidate as the active generated preview water path
do not claim Water plugin integration is validated for the preview river ribbon
do not overwrite the currently readable vertex-color water captures with the failed black-water captures

active_decision.decision: Use the opaque DefaultLit solver-surface parent and three river-specific instances on isolated Landscape candidates; retain the former Single Layer Water parent and instances as inactive evaluation evidence until production bathymetry and live-editor/package rendering can be revalidated.
active_decision.solver_surface_water_contract: Opaque DefaultLit surface shading uses river-specific surface/vertex tint, roughness, specular, seam-continuous dual-sampled first-party normal-atlas detail, and bounded Fresnel reflection fill. South Fork additionally samples clamped macro normals and packed RGBA depth/speed/Froude/detrended-eta generated from its accepted whole-window median finite-volume C++ frame. The same packed field drives noncolliding render-only surface relief and a separate noncolliding DefaultLit foam sheet with procedural breakup confined to the decoded hydraulic aeration mask. A movable skylight, runtime corridor reflection capture, and screen-space reflections serve the surface path without WaterBody authority or depth-dependent Single Layer volume composition.
active_decision.photographic_capture_contract: River-specific sun, skylight, fog, manual exposure, saturation, contrast, sharpening, and vignette values come from FRaftSimPhotographicCaptureSettings and are recorded per candidate. Camera film grain and engine-side capture dithering are both zero or disabled so capture detail must originate in rendered scene content.
active_decision.geometry_authority: The custom C++ water simulation remains authoritative. The solver visualization derivative changes only noncolliding review render geometry and material response under a 36 cm relief ceiling, feature forcing remains disabled, and neither it nor render-normal smoothing, render-only width overlap, foam opacity, or Fresnel fallback can change solver state, collision, raft forces, or conservation evidence.
active_decision.observed_result: Direct isolation captures proved that the procedural ribbon mesh is continuous and the lower-frame split came from Single Layer Water depth composition over provisional bathymetry: hiding the ribbon removed both zones, and swapping the same mesh to DefaultLit removed the split while preserving wave geometry. The active solver-surface material keeps that continuous response while retaining South Fork macro-normal, depth, speed, Froude, detrended free-surface relief, and field-confined foam; Colorado and Pacuare explicitly do not reuse those fields. Stronger wet-bank feather-tail shading did not move the residual bright bank strips, while bounded noncolliding render overlap did; 1.35x South Fork and 1.45x Pacuare widths remove continuous rails, with one isolated Pacuare bank point left for production microgeometry, while Colorado remains at 1.17x. Recorded photometry removes camera grain, and six river/type foliage material instances preserve visibly greener front/back/transmitted leaf response across all 3/3 audited twig slots per river. The result remains provisional because it lacks production reflections/refraction, live crest-scale turbulence, spray, validated bathymetry, bank transitions, atmosphere, and biome assets.
active_decision.promotion_gate: Do not promote the candidate until live-editor and packaged captures show credible reflection/refraction, solver-aligned flow detail, bank/riverbed transitions, foam and spray, hazard and swimmer/rescue readability, guide review, and measured desktop/VR performance for every seasonal flow band.

single_layer_depth_split_failure.summary: The purpose-built Single Layer Water candidate rendered two depth-dependent foreground zones over the provisional Landscape channel burn even though the procedural ribbon mesh itself was continuous.
isolation_evidence: hiding only RaftSim_ProceduralRiverRibbon removed both foreground zones
swapping the same ribbon to DefaultLit removed the split and preserved continuous wave geometry
normal-atlas seam crossfading and non-tiled composition did not move the split
changing preview channel depth moved the split, tying it to provisional bathymetry and volume composition
single_layer_depth_split_failure.decision: Keep Single Layer Water assets inactive until conditioned bathymetry and live-editor/package captures can prove continuous depth response.

next_actions: Extend the static South Fork review derivative to live per-frame solver displacement, velocity-aligned micro-normal advection, wet/dry transitions, crest-scale foam, and spray while keeping feature forcing disabled by default.
Generate Colorado and Pacuare fields only from their own accepted C++/GeoClaw scenarios; never reuse South Fork hydraulic textures across rivers.
Build production riverbed and wet-bank material transitions so shallow refraction does not expose coarse Landscape channel-burn geometry.
Replace the isolated Pacuare bank point with source-aligned production bank microgeometry; do not widen collision or add a synthetic cover.
Re-evaluate Single Layer Water only after conditioned bathymetry removes abrupt depth transitions; compare against the active solver-surface material in live editor and packaged builds.

Replace the now color-calibrated repeated PVE twig assemblies with biome-specific species geometry, age and crown diversity, seasonal state, and wind response while preserving the recorded leaf-color and transmission contract.
Replace the validation-grid foam sheet with solver-aligned crest-scale foam, spray, mist, and whitewater VFX for holes, wave trains, laterals, eddy lines, shelves, and boulder interactions.
Capture the same material in the live editor and packaged game because commandlet SceneCapture reflection behavior is not sufficient evidence for promotion.
Keep regenerated captures honest: this is a review candidate, not lifelike or production-approved water.
